docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Khaing Su Lwin

Independent Research Consultant

Water security | Climate Resilience | Nature-based Solutions | Southeast Asia

docs.title: About Me
docs.text:
I'm a researcher and engineer by training working at the intersection of water security, climate resilience, nature-based solutions, and environmental governance in Southeast Asia and the Mekong region. Most recently at Stockholm Environment Institute (SEI Asia) in Bangkok — first as a Fellow for Research to Policy and subsequently as a Research Consultant: my work focused on synthesising evidence and generating recommendations through systematic evidence mapping, qualitative research, and stakeholder engagement with researchers, governments, regional bodies, and development partners.

My recent work includes a first-authored, peer-reviewed publication in the Nature-Based Solutions journal. I bring qualitative research methods (NVivo, systematic mapping, key informant interviews), native Burmese language fluency, and a growing foundation in GIS and spatial analysis. I'm currently seeking researcher, analyst, and programme officer roles with regional organisations, INGOs, and think tanks working on water, climate resilience, or environmental governance in Asia.

I also use this space to think publicly and document my observations on topics that interest me beyond my research — ecology, urban life, and inclusivity - among others.

View My Projects Download CV

docs.title: Skills
docs.text:
• Research & Analysis

    • Systematic evidence mapping and literature review
    • Qualitative coding and analysis — NVivo
    • Key informant interviews and focus group design
    • Data collection, synthesis, and reporting

  • Research Communication

    • Peer-reviewed academic writing
    • Policy reports and synthesis reports
    • Blog writing and science communication
    • Podcast production and event facilitation

  • Stakeholder Engagement

    • Multi-stakeholder workshop design and facilitation
    • Community consultation
    • Regional network coordination
    • Youth engagement and capacity building

  • GIS & Spatial Analysis

    • QGIS (operational)
    • Python GIS programming (introductory — in progress)
    • Spatial analysis — MIT OpenCourseWare, Spatial Thoughts (ongoing)
    • Data visualisation — Datawrapper, Flourish

  • Technical Tools

    • Excel
    • Python (familiar — in progress)
    • Academic training — SWAT, HEC-RAS, ENVI
    • Reference management software

  • Languages

    • Burmese (native)
    • English (fluent)
    • French (A2–B1)

docs.title: Connect
docs.text: LinkedIn

docs.title: Contact
docs.text:
If my work resonates with you, or if you're working on something at the intersection of water, climate, nature-based solutions and research-policy interfaces, I'd love to hear from you. I'm currently open to research, analyst, and programme roles — but equally happy to just have a good conversation. The best way to reach me is by email.

Email khaingsulwin.lu@gmail.com GitHub github.com/khaingsulwin LinkedIn linkedin.com/in/khaing-su-lwin

Download CV

docs.title: Research Consultant — Stockholm Environment Institute (SEI) Asia
docs.text:
November 2024 – May 2026 | Remote / Bangkok, Thailand

  • Synthesised strategies and pathways for research-policy interfaces in the water-energy-climate nexus from 11 projects in the Mekong region and wrote-up a report
  • Published first-authored peer-reviewed article in Nature-Based Solutions journal on systematic mapping of NbS for water across the Greater Mekong Subregion
  • Conducted key informant interviews and focus group discussions; managed qualitative coding and analysis in NVivo

docs.title: Research Fellow — Stockholm Environment Institute (SEI) Asia
docs.text:
November 2022 – October 2024 | Bangkok, Thailand

  • Synthesised research outputs from over 30 multi-country projects using NVivo; produced policy reports and science communication outputs including blogs and podcast
  • Conducted systematic evidence mapping of nature-based solutions across the Mekong region using reference management software, Excel, Flourish, and Datawrapper
  • Co-managed regional young professionals network; facilitated regional research and policy event sessions

docs.title: Research & Data Analytics Intern — UNCDF (Blue Peace Financing)
docs.text:
April – October 2022 | Remote / New York, USA

  • Conducted thematic analysis on transboundary water institutions globally
  • Developed a living database of transboundary water basins and river basin organisations
  • Reviewed Blue Peace Index indicators to assess institutional readiness for financing

docs.title: Research Intern — International Water Management Institute (IWMI)
docs.text:
March – August 2021 | Yangon, Myanmar

  • Conducted literature review on water quality and aquaculture water management practices in Myanmar
  • Co-designed water quality measurement protocols and developed survey tools; conducted phone interviews with aquaculture farmers
  • Analysed data and submitted MSc thesis on water quality and small-scale aquaculture

docs.title: Research Intern — University of Science & Technology of Hanoi
docs.text:
July – September 2020 | Hanoi, Vietnam

  • Supported field sample collection of riverine sediments
  • Analysed physiochemical properties of river sediments in the laboratory
  • Built a results database in Excel

docs.title: Project Consultant — The Water Agency
docs.text:
December 2017 – August 2019 | Yangon, Myanmar

  • Organised and facilitated community consultations, multi-stakeholder workshops, and capacity building events for water and climate resilience themes
  • Liased with multi-stakeholders including governments, international companies, and universities
  • Synthesised water logistics sector data and supported management with business development
  • Assisted campaigns to grow Myanmar Water Portal and organised expo events

docs.title: Operations Engineer — Myanmar Industrial Port
docs.text:
2015 – 2017 | Yangon, Myanmar

  • Contributed port operation systems implementation and coordinated stakeholder feedback and end-user training

docs.title: Master of Water, Environment and Oceanography (Dual Degree)
docs.text:
University of Science and Technology of Hanoi & Université de Poitiers | 2021

Dual degree programme between Vietnam and France; thesis on water quality and small-scale aquaculture in Myanmar conducted in collaboration with IWMI.

docs.title: Bachelor of Engineering — Port and Harbor Engineering
docs.text: Myanmar Maritime University | 2015

docs.title: Certifications and Courses
docs.text:
• Introduction to GIS Programming — University of Tennessee,2026 (ongoing)
  • Spatial Analysis — Spatial Thoughts, 2026 (ongoing)
  • Introduction to Spatial Analysis — MIT OpenCourseWare, 2025
  • Methods in Systematic Review and Evidence Mapping — SEI / CEE, 2023
  • Water Resources Management and Policy - Université de Genève, 2020

docs.title: Journal Articles
docs.text:
1. Lwin, K. S., Saluja, R., Mandal, S. and Krittasudthacheewa, C. (2025). "Effectiveness of nature-based solutions to address water challenges in the Greater Mekong Subregion: a systematic mapping." Nature-Based Solutions, 8, 100283. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.nbsj.2025.100283

  2. Lwin, K. S., Saluja, R., Mandal, S., Lebel, L., Lebel, B., & Krittasudthacheewa, C. (2025). Interventions and pathways to reducing water insecurities in the Mekong region: insights from SUMERNET 4 All (S4A) research. SEI Report. https://doi.org/10.51414/sei2025.049

docs.title: Activities
docs.text:
• Panelist — Myanmar Climate Action Week, UN-Habitat, 2024

  • Session Facilitator — Annual Partner Meeting, UNESCAP, Sida, and Swedish Embassy, 2023

  • Rapporteur — Policy Platform for Climate Change Adaptation and Disaster Risk Management, ADB, 2022

docs.title: Theses

docs.title: Sample Notebook
docs.text:
In [1]: Copied! 
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import folium
import pandas as pd import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import folium In [ ]: Copied! 
# Placeholder time-series data
# Replace with: df = pd.read_csv("your_data.csv")
data = {
    "month": ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun",
              "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"],
    "metric_a": [120, 135, 148, 162, 175, 190, 185, 178, 160, 145, 130, 118],
    "metric_b": [ 80,  88,  95, 105, 112, 120, 118, 110, 100,  92,  85,  78],
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)
df.head()
# Placeholder time-series data # Replace with: df = pd.read_csv("your_data.csv") data = { "month": ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"], "metric_a": [120, 135, 148, 162, 175, 190, 185, 178, 160, 145, 130, 118], "metric_b": [ 80, 88, 95, 105, 112, 120, 118, 110, 100, 92, 85, 78], } df = pd.DataFrame(data) df.head() In [ ]: Copied! 
df[["metric_a", "metric_b"]].describe()
df[["metric_a", "metric_b"]].describe() In [4]: Copied! 
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))

ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_a"], marker="o", label="Metric A", color="steelblue")
ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_b"], marker="s", label="Metric B", color="coral")

ax.set_title("[YOUR CHART TITLE]", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")
ax.set_xlabel("Month")
ax.set_ylabel("[Unit]")
ax.legend()
ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4)) ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_a"], marker="o", label="Metric A", color="steelblue") ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_b"], marker="s", label="Metric B", color="coral") ax.set_title("[YOUR CHART TITLE]", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold") ax.set_xlabel("Month") ax.set_ylabel("[Unit]") ax.legend() ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5) plt.tight_layout() plt.show() In [7]: Copied! 
# Placeholder point locations
# Replace with your own lat/lon data, e.g. from a GeoDataFrame or CSV
sites = pd.DataFrame({
    "name":  ["Site A", "Site B", "Site C", "Site D", "Site E"],
    "lat":   [28.61,    28.65,    28.58,    28.70,    28.55],
    "lon":   [77.20,    77.23,    77.17,    77.28,    77.15],
    "value": [142,      168,      95,       210,       87],
})

# Centre the map on the mean location
m = folium.Map(
    location=[sites["lat"].mean(), sites["lon"].mean()],
    zoom_start=11,
    tiles="CartoDB positron",
)

# Add a CircleMarker for each site, scaled by value
for _, row in sites.iterrows():
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row["lat"], row["lon"]],
        radius=row["value"] / 20,          # scale radius to value
        color="steelblue",
        fill=True,
        fill_opacity=0.7,
        tooltip=f"{row['name']}: {row['value']}",
        popup=folium.Popup(
            f"<b>{row['name']}</b><br>Value: {row['value']}",
            max_width=200,
        ),
    ).add_to(m)

m
